_comment: RDR-014 codeIntel example. This is NOT a backends pool config (see coprocessor-pool-openrouter.example.json for that) — it is the per-spawn `opts` payload you pass to the qwen_spawn / qwen_oneshot MCP tools to give a coprocessor agent-lsp code intelligence. `codeIntel: true` synthesizes, server-side before the session starts: (1) an agent-lsp stdio MCP server under the reserved key 'agent-lsp', scoped via includeTools to the 10 high-signal read-only navigation tools; (2) a symbol-graph guidance block appended to the system prompt; (3) a max_tool_calls default of 12 (only if you do not set one). The tool scope is ENFORCED at MCP discovery (RDR-014 RF-4), not advisory. Prereq: `uvx` + `agent-lsp` on the coprocessor host (the supervisor does not install them) — verify with `agent-lsp doctor`. Security: agent-lsp launches at SDK init regardless of write_authority (RDR-013 trust model).

qwen_spawn_minimal._comment: Minimal form — one flag. cwd should point at the repo root agent-lsp indexes.
qwen_spawn_minimal.task: Find every caller of chooseBackend and summarize how each uses the result.

qwen_spawn_tuned._comment: Tuned form — larger budget for a refactor. A caller-set max_tool_calls always wins over the codeIntel default of 12; max_tool_calls: 0 means explicitly unbounded.
qwen_spawn_tuned.task: Rename the symbol `SpawnOpts` to `SessionOpts` across the package and fix all references.

_caller_wins_note: If you supply your own 'agent-lsp' key under opts.mcpServers, the supervisor keeps it untouched, logs a codeintel_lsp_key_present WARN, and suppresses the synthesized guidance (it describes agent-lsp's specific tools). The max_tool_calls default still applies unless you set one.

_synthesized_equivalent._comment: For reference only — this is roughly what `codeIntel: true` expands to internally (you do NOT write this yourself; the includeTools list and guidance are supplied by the supervisor). The bare tool names below are what `includeTools` matches on at MCP discovery.
